title: Galium emeryense
intro: Galium emeryense, commonly known as Emery's bedstraw, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to aid in the treatment of conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Galium emeryense include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Emery's bedstraw include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Galium emeryense is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the Rubus genus and is often classified within the tribe Rubieae. The plant is further classified under the order Gentianales and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Galium emeryense is also classified under the kingdom Plantae.
